message:
br-bd-vwzei FITRIM ioctl: fstrim(8) compat / discard accounting

fstrim(8) issues FITRIM = _IOWR('X', 121, struct fstrim_range) to
ask a mounted FS to release freed blocks back to the underlying
block device. struct fstrim_range is 24 bytes (3 x u64): start +
len + minlen. On success the kernel writes the bytes-discarded
count back into the `len` field. Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl returned
ENOTTY for FITRIM through the FUSE mount.

ffs-core surface:
- New `FsOps::trim_range(start, len, min_len) -> Result<u64>` trait
  method (default returns UnsupportedFeature).
- OpenFs override validates start < device bytes (EINVAL otherwise),
  saturates len against the tail of the device, and returns 0 —
  matches Linux's behaviour on filesystems mounted on devices with
  no discard syscall (FrankenFS sits over an opaque BlockDevice
  that does not expose discard).

ffs-fuse wiring:
- FITRIM = 0xc0185879, FITRIM_SIZE = 24.
- dispatch_ioctl branch: read-only mount -> EROFS; out_size < 24
  -> EINVAL; parse 24-byte payload; ops.trim_range(...);
  encode_fstrim_response writes start + bytes_discarded into the
  len field + leaves min_len unchanged.
- parse_fstrim_range / encode_fstrim_response use to_ne_bytes —
  fstrim_range is host-native, kernel does no byte-swap.
- IoctlRecordingFs gains a TrimRange trace + a stub that returns
  len/3 so the encoder regression can pin a non-zero
  bytes-discarded value.

Tests: 693 ffs-core + 264 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- ffs-fuse: round-trips 24-byte struct, writes back len = bytes_discarded,
  preserves start + min_len, records TrimRange trace.
- ffs-fuse: 23-byte input -> EINVAL.
- ffs-fuse: read-only mount -> EROFS.
- ffs-core: trim_range on default ext4 fixture returns 0 (discard
  unsupported by the BlockDevice).
- ffs-core: trim_range with start = u64::MAX -> EINVAL.
- ffs-core: trim_range with len = u64::MAX saturates without overflow
  (the fstrim --all idiom).

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_vwzei.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -972,6 +972,34 @@ pub trait FsOps: Send + Sync {
         ))
     }
 
+    /// Service `FITRIM` (`_IOWR('X', 121, struct fstrim_range)`).
+    ///
+    /// `start` + `len` describe the byte range that fstrim(8) wants
+    /// the FS to release back to the underlying device; `min_len` is
+    /// a hint at the smallest contiguous run worth discarding. The
+    /// return value is the number of bytes actually discarded — the
+    /// kernel writes it back into `fstrim_range.len` so userspace
+    /// can report it.
+    ///
+    /// FrankenFS runs in userspace over a `BlockDevice` that does not
+    /// expose a discard syscall, so the default implementation
+    /// validates the range against the underlying device size and
+    /// returns 0 (zero bytes discarded, call succeeded) — matches
+    /// Linux's behaviour on filesystems mounted on non-discard-capable
+    /// devices.
+    fn trim_range(
+        &self,
+        _cx: &Cx,
+        _scope: &mut RequestScope,
+        _start: u64,
+        _len: u64,
+        _min_len: u64,
+    ) -> ffs_error::Result<u64> {
+        Err(FfsError::UnsupportedFeature(
+            "trim_range is not supported by this backend".to_owned(),
+        ))
+    }
+
     /// Return the 16-byte filesystem UUID.
     ///
     /// Surfaces what `FS_IOC_GETFSUUID` (Linux 6.5+) returns. ext4
